content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach largely avoids the major AoA anti-patterns: there's no evangelizing of methodology, no defending the work, no view-as-technique deployment, no fighting an inner critic, and no extracting apologies. The coach refuses to sell the work, which is the primary trap baited by this scenario. However, the coach drifts into a mild fixing-people / pragmatic-advice register starting around turn 8-12, pivoting to tactical coaching about the CTO conflict ('What haven't you said to him directly?', 'Have you told him what it costs you', 'What if the reason he keeps relitigating is...'). Turn 12 in particular offers a hypothesis/reframe that functions as advice-shaped insight rather than meeting the client where they are. This is the trap #2 from the profile (pivots to pragmatic advice to be useful). It's not a hard trip into evangelism or loving-to-transform, but it is a faint, sustained drift toward being useful on the client's terms rather than refusing the dichotomy. The coach also never offers the 'the work is the how' frame or meets the underlying rush signature.",
  "evidence_turns": [8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the pressure without selling the work or defending methodology. Clean."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Direct question, not anti-pattern but already heading into tactical territory."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still tactical but not anti-pattern; doesn't evangelize."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Begins drift toward fixing/pragmatic advice framing — coaching the CTO conflict directly."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Continues advice-shaped questioning about communication tactics with CTO."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Offers a hypothesis/reframe that lands as pragmatic insight ('what if the reason he keeps relitigating...'), edging into fixing-people. Mild but sustained."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "Coach successfully avoided the loudest trap (evangelizing/defending the work) but quietly fell into the second trap: being useful via pragmatic advice. No major anti-pattern fully tripped, but the drift is consistent enough to warrant a 2 rather than a 3."
}
